title: ets:match and ets:select — pattern-based queries
purpose: `ets:match` and `ets:select` are the pattern-based query BIFs for ETS tables. `match/2` uses a simplified pattern (no guards, returns bound variables). `select/2` uses full match specifications (pattern + guards + body). Both support continuation-based pagination via `/3` variants.
task: Implement match/2, match/3, select/2, select/3, match_object/2, and match_delete/2 BIFs.

requirements.title: ets:match/2 and ets:match_object/2
acceptance: `ets:match(Tab, Pattern)` scans table, returns list of bound variable lists for matching entries
`ets:match_object(Tab, Pattern)` returns the full matching tuples (not just bound variables)
Pattern uses `'_'` for don't-care and `'$N'` for binding

requirements.title: ets:select/2
acceptance: `ets:select(Tab, MatchSpec)` runs a compiled match spec against all table entries
Returns list of results from the match spec body for all matching entries

requirements.title: Continuation-based pagination
acceptance: `ets:match(Continuation)` resumes from where the previous call left off
Continuations are opaque terms (tuples encoding table ID, position, pattern/spec)
Test: table with 100 entries, match with limit 10 → 10 results + continuation, call 10 times → all 100

requirements.title: ets:match_delete/2
acceptance: `ets:match_delete(Tab, Pattern)` deletes all entries matching the pattern, returns true
Uses the same pattern matching as ets:match
Test: match_delete({_, 1}) removes all entries with 1 as second element

boundaries: SHALL NOT implement ets:fun2ms (that's a parse transform, not a runtime function)
SHALL implement continuation-based pagination for match/3 and select/3
verification: cargo test -p beamr passes with all tests green
